title: comastoma pulmonarium subsp. sectum
intro: Comastoma pulmonarium subsp. sectum, also known as Lungwort, is a species of flowering plant in the gentian family, native to mountainous regions in Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Comastoma pulmonarium subsp. sectum include reducing congestion, relieving coughs, and soothing irritated airways. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, often combined with other herbs for enhanced benefits.
